title: achillea millefolium var. pacifica
intro: Achillea millefolium var. pacifica, also known as Western Yarrow, is a perennial plant species native to the Pacific coast of North America, characterized by its fern-like foliage and clusters of small white or yellow flowers. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as wounds, digestive problems, and menstrual cramps. The health benefits of Achillea millefolium var. pacifica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate menstrual discomfort, making it a popular herbal remedy. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used internally or applied topically to the skin to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
